detailedExemplarData.title: How did Martin Luther Kingâs speeches reverberate in South Africa and the United States and how did they contribute to antiracism, such as going against the apartheid during the mid 20th century (1948 - 1994) and how does their impact compare with the speeches of Nelson Mandela ?

subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: The topic is communicated clearly throughout with explicit headings and signposts reinforcing the line of inquiry.
Key terms and temporal frame are defined and contextualized, giving the investigation clear purpose and focus.
The research question is stated and revisited, maintaining consistent connection to the analysis.
weaknesses: The research question remains somewhat broad, attempting to cover multiple dimensions and diluting tighter focus.
Methodological rationale (selection criteria for speeches) is implied rather than explicitly outlined.
Sentence-level phrasing is occasionally wordy or imprecise, leading to moments of vagueness in the stated aims.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission consistently communicates its topic and purpose at a level commensurate with Band 3-4. Clear signposting and contextual definitions maintain focus, but the breadth of the research question and uneven methodological exposition prevent a fully razor-sharp approach. A mid-band score of 4 reflects strong clarity and organization with room to tighten focus and explicitly frame the methodology.

strengths: Demonstrates solid historical and contextual knowledge of both apartheid and the U.S. Civil Rights Movement, enriching the analysis.
Effective integration of quotations and primary text examples to support claims in most sections.
Terminology such as ethos, pathos, metaphor, and allusion is applied accurately in key analyses.
weaknesses: Some source integration remains descriptive, with occasional reliance on tertiary web pages that limits scholarly depth.
Use of rhetorical terminology is at times mechanical; complex concepts (e.g. logos) are under-explored.
Minor citation errors and imprecise attributions detract from consistent academic rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows clear, coherent understanding of topic and sources and employs subject-specific terminology correctly most of the time, placing the work in Band 3-4. While knowledge is solid and terminology largely accurate, occasional imprecision and source weaknesses prevent a higher band. A score of 4 reflects effective but not yet exemplary knowledge and use of concepts.

strengths: Research encompasses a broad range of primary and secondary materials that are largely relevant to the research question.
Analysis identifies key rhetorical devices and links them to their effects, particularly in the comparative section.
Argument is coherent, with clear parallels drawn between Kingâs and Mandelaâs strategies, demonstrating evaluative intent.
weaknesses: Depth of evaluation is limited: impact claims are asserted without nuanced evidence or counter-perspectives.
Conclusion tends to restate findings rather than critically reflect on limitations or broader implications.
Certain analytic threads (e.g. deeper schematic patterns or audience reception theory) remain underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance aligns with Band 4-6. The work advances a coherent argument supported by relevant research and sound analysis, yet deeper critical evaluation and consideration of limitations are lacking. Assigning the top of this band (6) reflects consistent, good-quality critical thinking with identifiable areas for further depth.

strengths: Structure follows all required Extended Essay conventionsâcover page, contents, introduction, body sections, conclusion, and works citedâproviding clear navigability.
Layout and formatting (headings, subheadings, page numbers) are consistently applied, aiding reader orientation.
Typographic hierarchy and consistent use of headings reinforce the logical flow of the argument.
weaknesses: Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent citation style and broken URLs, affecting professionalism.
Some section headings lack uniform formatting, and a few major headings in the table of contents miss page numbers.
Typographical errors and uneven section lengths interrupt the polish expected at this level.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all formal requirements and applies layout conventions correctly, placing it in Band 3-4. Despite some inconsistencies in citation formatting and minor structural glitches, the essay is well organized and reader-friendly. A top-band score of 4 recognizes sturdy fulfillment of presentation expectations with scope to refine details.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student selects four primary texts (two speeches by each leader) and supports the commentary with a works-cited list of both primary and secondary sources, showing some measure of informed choice. Basic rhetorical analysis is applied consistently, and contextual information is supplied for each speech.

Nonetheless, the methodology is largely descriptive; there is no explicit rationale for how or why the specific speeches were chosen over others, and the analytical framework (e.g., rhetorical criticism, discourse analysis) is implied rather than systematically outlined. Source selection skews heavily toward easily accessible web articles, with limited evidence of rigorous academic filtering. These features suggest a mostly complete but not fully robust method, aligning with Band 3-4 at the lower end (score 3).

The submission includes key formal elements: title page details, word count, table of contents with page references, and a works-cited section. The layout is generally clear, with consistent use of headings and typographic hierarchy. Page numbers are indicated in the contents table and appear to be present in the document.

Formatting inconsistencies are noticeableâcitation style fluctuates, some URLs are long and not uniformly presented, and occasional typographical errors detract from professionalism. Nonetheless, the essential requirements are met and applied correctly overall, situating the work within Band 3-4 at a solid score of 3.

The student cites approximately thirty sources, including academic journals, reputable news outlets, and primary texts. Most references are pertinent to the research questionâcovering the speeches themselves, historical background, and scholarly commentary on rhetoricâindicating **good breadth and reasonable relevance**. In-text citations and a works-cited list are present, though formatting inconsistencies occur.

While the majority of sources contribute meaningfully, some are generic web materials (e.g., Merriam-Webster, Distance Calculator) that add little scholarly value. The research base thus fulfils the descriptor for Band 4-6 (âadequate â¦ with partial relevanceâ) but shows enough range and alignment with the inquiry to merit a mid-band score of 5 rather than the minimal 4.

The student states the topic explicitly in the title and reiterates it in the opening paragraph, establishing a clear intention to compare the persuasive techniques of Martin Luther King Jr. and Nelson Mandela. Key terms such as âpublic speaking,â âantiracism,â and the temporal frame âmid-20th century (1948-1994)â are defined and contextualised, allowing the reader to understand the scope of the investigation.

Although sentence-level accuracy is uneven and the topic statement is wordy, the overall focus remains identifiable throughout the essayâs structure (introduction, individual analyses, comparative section, conclusion). Headings and signposts reinforce the line of inquiry, and the reader is rarely left in doubt about what is being explored. These features satisfy the descriptors of Band 3-4, with sufficient clarity and purpose, but the occasional vagueness and breadth of the stated aims prevent the consistently sharp focus required for the top band.

The student advances a coherent line of argument that Kingâs and Mandelaâs rhetorical strategies galvanized resistance to racism. The discussion touches on historical impact and draws parallels between the two contexts, indicating **some evaluative intent**.

However, evaluative depth is limited: claims about the speeches âleading to the downfall of apartheidâ are asserted without nuanced evidence, and counter-perspectives are absent. The conclusion mainly restates earlier points rather than critically reflecting on implications or limitations. The work therefore satisfies Band 4-6 for âgood argument with partially critical evaluation,â earning a score of 4.

The analysis moves beyond summary by identifying rhetorical devices (pathos, ethos, metaphor) and linking them to effects; quotations are discussed rather than merely listed. Comparative comments between King and Mandela appear in the dedicated section and conclusion, showing an attempt at synthesis.

Nevertheless, much of the commentary remains descriptive, with limited exploration of deeper stylistic patterns, audience reception, or counter-arguments. Connections to the broader research question (impact on antiracism in two regions) are asserted more than demonstrated with evidence. These factors align with the âadequate analysis with partially supported conclusionsâ of Band 4-6, meriting a score of 4.

The essay follows the required EE structure: title page, abstract substitute (implicit), table of contents, introduction, body organised by speech, comparative analysis, conclusion, and works cited. Headings and sub-headings are used effectively, and the sequence is logical, enabling the reader to follow the argument.

Minor issuesâsuch as some uneven section lengths, occasional redundancy, and the absence of explicit methodology or reflection sections within the main essayâprevent it from demonstrating the polished integration expected for the top mark. These characteristics accord with the lower end of Band 3-4, so a score of 3 is appropriate.

The essay displays **solid knowledge of both historical contexts and the chosen speeches**. Background on apartheid, the U.S. Civil Rights Movement and biographical details of King and Mandela are generally accurate and help frame the analysis. Quotations are plentiful and usually relevant, and a sizeable bibliography indicates engagement with a range of secondary material.

However, the use of sources is not always fully integrated: some references are descriptive, and occasional factual imprecision and over-reliance on tertiary webpages limit authority. The studentâs understanding is clear but not consistently sophisticated, fitting the upper end of Band 3-4 (score 4) rather than the consistently âexcellentâ standard of Band 5-6.

The student employs key rhetorical termsâethos, pathos, metaphor, historical allusionâand applies them correctly in most cases, demonstrating awareness of subject-specific concepts relevant to English Language & Literature. This places the work within Band 3-4.

Yet the terminology is applied somewhat mechanically and not always with precision; for example, complex concepts such as ethos are occasionally simplified, and logos is scarcely addressed despite its relevance. Some rhetorical terminology (e.g., antithesis, anaphora) is mentioned only briefly or overlooked. Consequently, the use of terms is âmostly accurateâ but lacks the consistency required for Band 5-6, warranting a score of 3.

The research question is explicitly presented and revisited, demonstrating awareness of its centrality. However, it attempts to cover several dimensions at once (reverberations in two countries, contribution to antiracism, comparison of impact, and stylistic techniques), making it broader than ideal. In practice the essay concentrates mainly on rhetorical strategies and some aspects of historical influence, so the connection between the multifaceted question and the ensuing discussion is only partial.

Because the question is clear but not tightly delimitedâand the linkage between every part of the question and the analysis sometimes weakâthe strand fits Band 3-4 (âclearly stated but only partially focused and connectedâ), meriting a mid-band score of 3 rather than the upper value.

annotations.comment: The historical overview of segregation provides context but remains general. Integrate specific primary text examples or data points to strengthen evidence of your understanding.
The explanation of how emotive language like âindiscriminate slaughterâ evokes sympathy demonstrates clear understanding of pathos and its impact on audience perception.
Many URL citations in the bibliography appear broken or truncated. Ensure URLs are complete, clickable, and formatted according to the selected citation style.

The research question attempts to address reverberations, antiracism contributions, and comparative impact simultaneously, making it overly broad. Narrow the focus or break into subquestions to ensure each dimension is thoroughly explored and tightly connected to the analysis.
Analysis of Kingâs historical allusions effectively shows logos and pathos interplay, but the conclusion of this analytical thread is cut off. Complete the synthesis of effect and significance.
The comparative analysis opening clearly establishes the shared rhetorical strategies of ethos and pathos, setting up a focused cross-text evaluation.
The restated research question mirrors the introduction verbatim, which can feel repetitive. Paraphrase and tighten the wording to reinforce focus and avoid redundancy.
The identification of Mandelaâs use of positive utopian expressions effectively illustrates his strategic ethos-building and optimism during the transition speech.
The overview of Kingâs London speech and its historical context is succinct and demonstrates good knowledge of cross-continental civil rights connections.
Consider adding an explicit reflection section discussing your decision-making, challenges, and personal engagement to address the EEâs reflection requirement.
A Mandela quote is attributed to Martin Luther King, indicating a citation error. Verify all source attributions to maintain credibility and accuracy in referencing.
The transition to the Martin Luther King section lacks a parallel structural heading style. Use consistent formatting to signal shifts between major case studies.
The inserted definition of pathos disrupts narrative flow. Consider relocating definitional material to an early methodology section or integrating more succinctly.
The word count is provided and meets EE requirements, demonstrating adherence to the formatting guidelines.
Several major sections in the table of contents lack page numbers, hindering navigation. Ensure all headings and subheadings are accompanied by accurate page references for clarity.
Comparative analysis within Mandelaâs speeches is limited here. Introduce explicit contrasts between rhetorical strategies in his trial and inauguration speeches to highlight shifts in approach.
The contextual background on post-apartheid negotiations and Mandelaâs release is accurate and enriches the analysis of his ethos construction in the American context.
Quotation numbering resets inconsistently in this list. Standardize numbering or bullet formatting to maintain clear organization and reader orientation.
Biblical references are used to build ethos but lack precise citation details inline (e.g., chapter or verse). Provide fuller attributions to strengthen scholarly rigor.
The heading for âLet My People Goâ is precise, but the section would benefit from a brief explanation of selection criteria to clarify methodology behind speech choices.
The metaphor analysis linking apartheid to Nazism is powerful and well-supported, effectively using historical allusion to heighten audience empathy.
The quote âHumbled and elevated by the honorâ demonstrates ethos but would benefit from analysis of how this aligns with audience values and expectations in the 1990s U.S. political climate.
The âImpactâ section reads as summary rather than evaluative discussion. Develop a critical assessment of how each leaderâs techniques translated into concrete social change outcomes.
The conclusion reiterates main findings but adds little new insight. Introduce reflections on limitations or suggestions for further research to demonstrate deeper critical evaluation.
The âWorks Citedâ heading is clearly formatted and stands out, aiding the reader in locating the bibliography section effectively.
The first bibliographic entry spans multiple lines and includes inconsistent punctuation. Consolidate each entry into a single paragraph with uniform style and punctuation.
The section header âNelson Mandelaâ is clear, but a brief methodological outline at the start of this section would reinforce your approach and link analysis back to research objectives.
While the title indicates a comparison of public speaking techniques, specifying the subjects (King and Mandela) would enhance clarity and immediately orient the reader to the essayâs scope.
The in-text citation â(Aldosari 6)â lacks a publication year. Adopt a consistent citation style throughout to ensure each reference includes author, year, and page details.

The discussion of pathos convincingly explains emotional appeal but remains descriptive. Deepen analysis by linking specific terms to audience response theories or situating them within broader rhetorical frameworks.
